item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*sees the number choice*
*squints*
*notices sPC*
"AH okay."

Anyway, this is good. You get an upvotepercut.

item.title: Re: How did I come up with an SPC format?
item.content:encoded:
Jacob Conwell, I would think. However there are other authors, Doctor Cimmerian has used them twice ('Rogue AI' (NVEO4/YVN3E/PN6UG) and SCP-2987) also there's nothing in Zyn's post about multiple authors although that may be assumed.

Comparatively the SPC has ten tales, three mainlist articles, one format and two -Js by nine different authors.

item.title: Re: How did I come up with an SPC format?
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure how many people have used Anderson and the requisites for "official" hubs. I mean, I know there's the android and the cognitohazard skips (and one passing mention in a certain article's history, but I won't spoil that here), but I think the bulk of its use are tales by… was it Fahrenheit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am sincerely honoured by this, since I intended to be bold with this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Passing through again on my rate and comment every contest entry run, and I just wanted to say that this is the best entry from this team and probably the boldest successful article in the contest. This takes an utterly ridiculous premise and makes it really work and even fairly scary. This is probably my second favorite goi-format article in the contest.

item.title: Re: How did I come up with an SPC format?
item.content:encoded:
Gracious, I hope not.

I am not entirely sure that I can be stopped now.

item.title: Re: How did I come up with an SPC format?
item.content:encoded:
I think that I was thinking of the GOI guide— Zyn says she thinks it is three SCPs or five tales, here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-76859/groups-of-interest#post-2403394, though there are some other GOIs (Anderson certainly) that aren't on the list but meet the requirement.

The SPC already had three mainlist SCPs, and now a successful format.

item.title: Re: How did I come up with an SPC format?
item.content:encoded: At this point, for GoIs that don't have an official hub yet, all you need is an article that stays afloat.

item.title: Re: How did I come up with an SPC format?
item.content:encoded:
Gracious, I hope not. I might, just to do the format a bit more justice than it deserves.

And I think there's no ruling over the prerequisites for how a supposed GoI format transits to an official GoI format…

item.title: Re: How did I come up with an SPC format?
item.content:encoded: So now it needs two more articles by two more authors to be established as a format?

item.title: How did I come up with an SPC format?
item.content:encoded: Since the contest is over and by Decibelles' ruling that freshly made GoI formats should have some explanation, here's mine: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-1598961/spc-2615#post-2457250

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I'm aware of those three (which ultimately convinced me that SPC-related anomalies can be done). I guess the extra-universal element is kinda a given, since it made the most amount of sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like it's worth mentioning there are three mainlist SPC skips, and they're all quite serious. Though they also tend to treat the SPC as extra-universal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goodness no. They were merely my headcanon notes on the Centre/SPC (independent of this article). SPC-2615 is but the many projects that sprung out from their 001 entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So a stupid SPC that makes people think there's supernatural sharks everywhere, explains the origin of the SPC and reveals that the writer of the SCP foundation actually created it on accident.

I…I literally can't describe what I'm feeling here. It's a strange mixture of confusion and amazement, with a bit of hatred and wish to punch Selachian entities in the face.

Edit: HOLY SHIT THE AUTO CORRECT ON THAT WAS SO BOTCHED BEFORE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So we accidentally the whole universe. Ok.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If we go by Swann's proposal (which I believe you have ambivalent feelings towards), we are the reason why the Foundation-multiverse exists.

And isn't this site the ones who made "SPC" a thing and equate it to a peculiar institution of punching sharks?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what you're saying is, we accidentally the whole universe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shirt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If God is a bunch of fiction writers, what happens if they did a typo in their word processor that was not corrected?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit.

Hats off to you man, you actually managed to give the SPC a sort of meta in universe purpose. Fucking hell XD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, so SPC-001 is meant to be the origin of the SPC joke, and because of that it's forcing IC people to make it real in universe? H-hot damn man.

From my notes on SPC from my cranium:

+ show block
– hide block
  • SPC-001 is indeed the in-universe origin of the Centre and its actions.
  • Out-of-universe, it can be deemed as the origin of the SPC joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, so SPC-001 is meant to be the origin of the SPC joke, and because of that it's forcing IC people to make it real in universe? H-hot damn man.

+1 I've always had a soft spot for SPC by the way, the concept makes me chuckle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On the other hand this was a entertaining read in the whole "wat" sense, especially the revelations in the final interview.

+ show block
– hide block

While I have never stated what is the SPC-001 they're referring to, the interview's last line "We're not in control" is a reference to Mann's proposal (that the Centre is equally not in control of events; and the out-of-universe SPC is created by events beyond the fiction's control).

And that note, fictional characters never have any control over how they should be like. Always on the whims of their writers and authors. Which makes it similar to Swann's proposal.

P.S. SPC is a dumb concept. But that doesn't mean it should go to conceptual Hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this was odd. And has left me torn. On one hand, I personally find the entire concept of the SPC to be kind of, well, dumb. On the other hand this was a entertaining read in the whole "wat" sense, especially the revelations in the final interview.

Tentative +1 for now. I am still pretty confused, but it is an amusing confusing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, derp, I didn't realize the thingy in "Finfiction" was this. I'm dumb, haha, carry on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This, somehow, works… I don't understand, and I am afraid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure when I'll be able to stop giggling at "And here we are, punching shar-". Probably not anytime this century.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still don't know what it was missing, but it has it now. +1

item.title: Thanks, Attribution, Formatting and Others
item.content:encoded:
So this is the GOI format for my team in this contest. Yes, it's about the SPC (or the Centre, as I like to call it) but they got three mainlisters and classified as a minor GOI on the O5 Tags guide.

Allow me to thank everyone whom I bugged on the chat to look though: thedeadlymoose, TL333s, A Random Day, Lex1nat0r, Oboebandgeek99, JackIke and so on.

The anomalous entity featured here is taken and derived from:

  • Finfiction by TobiasTheTapir

The following is about the SPC format, and other notes.

+ show block
– hide block

A few common trends about the reality/realities where the SPC is set in (at least via this format's interpretation):

  • Firstly, this GoI would refer to itself as the "Centre" a la "Foundation" and "SCP Foundation". "Shark Punching Center/Centre" (in-universe) is more of a nickname by dissenters/the Centre's list of GoIs.1
  • Contrary to the SCP world existing in "our reality",2 the worlds where the Centre exist surely have more divergences than just one anomaly-dealing organisation. Go full-on alternate history here. For instance, in SPC-2615, I use the small mention of a "Pan-Celtic Union". Distance the setting from our history.
  • The Centre deals with the so-called Selachian entities, which are essentially sharks. Some are really anomalous (Deviant-Type); some are still normal sharks (Baseline-Type). They're all targeted all the same. In the Centre's eyes, punching them is worth all the trouble and that is deemed to be a higher priority than the existence of anomalies (attributed to SPC-001).
  • Contrary to SCP Foundation's policy of multiple SCP-001 proposals, the Centre has a singular SPC-001. SPC-001 is known to all members of the Centre, to justify their methods. Like many of the SCP-001 proposals, SPC-001 is a cause for the Centre's existence.
  • Overall, the Centre is not all that different from the Foundation. While most GoIs are marked by their differences from the Foundation, this GoI is uncannily similar to the Foundation. Yet, the two groups have a different outlook on things.

NOTICE FROM THE CENTRAL INTELLIGENCE COORDINATION AND PROJECTS OPERATION COMMAND OFFICE

[The CICAPOCO3 is functionally a counterpart to the Foundation's RAISA or CI's DeCIRO. For every SPC article, CICAPOCO would issue notes to indicate if the project is active, on hiatus, terminated etc. If the project is not active, it will list conditions where said project may resume operation.]

Project #: SPC-[INSERT NUMBER]4

Selachian Pugnātorial Capabilities: [To the Centre, anomalies are kept for the purpose of dealing with Selachian entities. Detail how your anomaly-based project can aid in the Centre's objectives.]

Project Component(s): [The counterpart for Description. The most notable difference would be that the Centre is more willing to combine anomalies together for a single project. Be clear and list out the various components of the project in question, and why the Centre is interested in them. Of course, not every component has to be anomalous. Some can be mundane everyday tools, repurposed for the Centre's goals. Heck, there can be projects with no anomalous objects as components.]

Augmentation Summary: [Not all anomalies are intended to deal with Selachian entities. Here, the Centre will detail what they did to the anomalies acquired to make it compatible with the Centre's objectives. Feel free to go into detail what the Centre did.]

Deployment Record: [This is where the Centre records the effectiveness of their projects out on the field. It can range from AARs of fights against Selachian entities, or interviews, or surveys, or test logs etc. Whatever that suits your article's purpose, really.]

Footnotes
1. Which is also my headcanon for the term "SCP Foundation".
2. Or a world that geopolitically/societally resembles our own.
3. Acronym, because it is too long to be spoken.
4. The number can be a reference to SCPs from the mainlist. For example, SPC-2615 is a reference to SCP-2615 (as an alternate universe counterpart; think Marvel Multiverse here). It's not a rule, and authors can choose whatever number they please.
